perturbation.original_text: 1. DEFINITIONS. For purposes of this Agreement, the following expressions shall have the meanings given to them below: 1.1 "Affiliate" means any company which is directly or indirectly controlled by BKC, controls BKC, or is controlled by a company which in turn controls BKC, and "control" for these purposes means de facto control. 1.2 "Burger King Marks" has the meaning ascribed to it in Paragraph A of the introduction. 1.3 "Burger King System" has the meaning ascribed to it in Paragraph A of the introduction. 1.4 "Current Image" means the then current, BKC approved physical appearance of new Burger King Restaurants as it relates to signage, fascia, color schemes, menu boards, lighting, furniture, finishes and other non-structural matters generally. 1.5 "Franchised Restaurant" means the buildings at the Location and the business carried out at the Location. 1.6 "Gross Sales" has the meaning ascribed to it in Subparagraph 9.3. 1.7 "Location" has the meaning ascribed to it on SCHEDULE 1. 2 1.8 "Director of Operations" has the meaning ascribed to it in Subparagraph 4.1 and on SCHEDULE 1. 1.9 "Managing Director" has the meaning ascribed to it in Subparagraph 4.3 and on SCHEDULE 1. 1.10 "MOD Manual" means all volumes of the Manual of Operating Data setting out BKC's standards, specifications and procedures of operation, as revised from time to time by BKC including both required and recommended.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. DEFINITIONS. For purposes of this Agreement, the following expressions shall have the meanings given to them below: 1.1 "Affiliate" means any company which is directly or indirectly controlled by BKC, controls BKC, or is controlled by a company which in turn controls BKC, and "control" for these purposes means influence. 1.2 "Burger King Marks" has the meaning ascribed to it in Paragraph A of the introduction. 1.3 "Burger King System" has the meaning ascribed to it in Paragraph A of the introduction. 1.4 "Current Image" means a generally appealing aesthetic. 1.5 "Franchised Restaurant" means the buildings at the Location and the business carried out at the Location. 1.6 "Gross Sales" has the meaning ascribed to it in Subparagraph 9.3. 1.7 "Location" has the meaning ascribed to it on SCHEDULE 1. 2 1.8 "Director of Operations" has the meaning ascribed to it in Subparagraph 4.1 and on SCHEDULE 1. 1.9 "Managing Director" has the meaning ascribed to it in Subparagraph 4.3 and on SCHEDULE 1. 1.10 "MOD Manual" means operational recommendations.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the definitions of 'Affiliate,' 'Current Image,' and 'MOD Manual' introduces ambiguity and contradiction within the contract. Changing 'de facto control' to 'influence' for 'Affiliate' weakens the definition, making it unclear what level of control constitutes an affiliate. Defining 'Current Image' as 'a generally appealing aesthetic' instead of the BKC-approved specific elements introduces subjectivity and contradicts the sections requiring strict adherence to BKC standards. Redefining 'MOD Manual' as 'operational recommendations' removes the mandatory aspect, conflicting with clauses that mandate compliance with the MOD Manual's standards and procedures. For example, Section 5.1 states 'Strict Compliance' with the Burger King System, which includes adhering to the MOD Manual's specifications. The change makes it uncertain whether the franchisee is legally obligated to follow these recommendations, creating a contradiction in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. STANDARDS AND UNIFORMITY. 5.1 STRICT COMPLIANCE. The Franchisee agrees to comply strictly at all times with the Burger King System, which Franchisee acknowledges is a fundamental term of this Agreement and a necessary and reasonable requirement in the interests of the Franchisee and others operating under the Burger King System. In particular, the Franchisee shall at all times comply with the following provisions of this Section 5.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces strong in-text contradictions by weakening the definitions of key terms, specifically 'Affiliate', 'Current Image', and 'MOD Manual'. This directly conflicts with Section 5.1, which mandates 'STRICT COMPLIANCE' with the 'Burger King System,' including adherence to the MOD Manual's standards. By changing the MOD Manual from a set of strict standards to mere 'operational recommendations,' the modified text creates uncertainty about the franchisee's legal obligation to follow specific procedures. The changed text makes compliance open to interpretation, thus creating a direct contradiction with the original requirement of 'STRICT COMPLIANCE'.

perturbation.original_text: 5.16 SOURCES OF SUPPLY. 5.16.1 AUTHORIZED SUPPLIERS. BKC may require that any item required for or used in the operation of the Franchised Restaurant shall be previously approved by BKC in its sole and absolute discretion and that the supplier and distributor of such items also be previously approved by BKC in its sole and absolute discretion. The Franchisee shall in such case purchase only from BKC authorized suppliers and distributors. Should the Franchisee propose an alternative supplier and distributor, BKC shall evaluate such supplier and distributor against its then-current criteria, as established by BKC in its sole discretion, and either approve or disapprove such supplier and distributor. Any supplier and distributor proposed by the Franchisee may be required to sign a suitable confidentiality undertaking before BKC's confidential specifications are disclosed. In approving or disapproving suppliers and distributors, the Franchisee acknowledges and agrees that BKC may devote such resources and time as BKC may reasonably determine is necessary to evaluate any such supplier or distributor in its sole discretion. BKC agrees that it will apply those criteria in good faith toward the Franchisee. If BKC denies the Franchisee's request for approval of a supplier or distributor BKC shall advise the Franchisee of the reasons for its decision. If BKC fails to approve or deny the Franchisee's 8 request for approval of a supplier or distributor within thirty (30) business days then approval shall be deemed to have been given. Approval of any suppler or distributor by BKC is subject to revocation in its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.16 SOURCES OF SUPPLY. 5.16.1 AUTHORIZED SUPPLIERS. The Franchisee may purchase supplies from any supplier, provided they meet basic health and safety standards. Should the Franchisee propose an alternative supplier and distributor, BKC may choose to evaluate such supplier and distributor. The Franchisee acknowledges and agrees that BKC may devote resources and time to evaluate such supplier or distributor. If BKC denies the Franchisee's request for approval of a supplier or distributor BKC may advise the Franchisee of the reasons for its decision. Approval of any suppler or distributor by BKC is subject to revocation in its discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying Section 5.16.1, the stringent control BKC has over suppliers is significantly weakened. Removing BKC's 'sole and absolute discretion' in approving suppliers and distributors, and allowing franchisees to purchase from any supplier meeting 'basic health and safety standards,' contradicts the clause requiring adherence to the MOD Manual and BKC's specifications for supplies. This creates an in-text contradiction, as franchisees are simultaneously bound by product specifications (requiring specific suppliers) and free to choose their own suppliers (provided they meet basic standards). Section 5.7 states 'Only food, paper products, packaging and supplies from sources approved by BKC shall be used.' This removal creates ambiguity over which standard prevails
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.7 MENU, SERVICE AND HYGIENE. The Franchised Restaurant shall serve all menu items and brands specified by BKC, and shall not serve any items that are not set forth in the MOD Manual or otherwise authorized and approved by BKC in writing. The Franchisee shall adhere to all specifications contained in the MOD Manual or as otherwise prescribed by BKC as to ingredients, storage, handling, method of preparation and service, weight and dimensions of products served, and standards of cleanliness, health, and sanitation. All food, drinks, and other items will be served and sold in packaging that meets BKC's specifications. Only food, paper products, packaging and supplies from sources approved by BKC (which expression includes sources of both product and distribution) shall be used in the Franchised Restaurant.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.16.1 significantly weakens BKC's control over suppliers, allowing franchisees to purchase from any supplier meeting 'basic health and safety standards.' This directly contradicts Section 5.7, which mandates that 'Only food, paper products, packaging and supplies from sources approved by BKC...shall be used.' The original text of 5.16.1 clearly states that BKC has sole and absolute discretion over suppliers. The removal of this discretion and the introduction of 'basic health and safety standards' creates a clear and direct conflict between the two sections, making it an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 17.2 TERMINATION. Upon the occurrence of an Event of Default, this Agreement shall automatically terminate without any further notice or opportunity to cure under Section 17.1.1 above and BKC shall, subject to the provisions of Subsection 17.6 below, have the right to claim lost royalties and advertising contributions, and shall also have all other rights and remedies available under applicable law. Upon the occurrence of an Event of BKC Default under Section 17.1.2., this Agreement shall automatically terminate without further notice or opportunity to cure and the Franchisee shall have all other 29 rights and remedies available under applicable law. Subject to the provisions of Section 17.6 below, the rights of the parties set forth in this Section 17.2 shall be in addition to any other rights the parties may have under applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: 17.2 TERMINATION. Upon the occurrence of an Event of Default, this Agreement shall terminate, with BKC reserving all rights under this agreement. Upon the occurrence of an Event of BKC Default under Section 17.1.2., this Agreement shall terminate and the Franchisee shall have all other rights and remedies available under applicable law. Subject to the provisions of Section 17.6 below, the rights of the parties set forth in this Section 17.2 shall be in addition to any other rights the parties may have under applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'automatically terminate without any further notice or opportunity to cure' creates ambiguity regarding the termination process. While stating the 'agreement shall terminate,' omitting the automatic termination clause suggests BKC may need to provide notice or an opportunity to cure, which directly contradicts the detailed Events of Default outlined in section 17.1.1, implying immediate termination for certain breaches. This change would create an in-text contradiction with the subsequent sections detailing post-termination obligations, as it's now unclear under what precise conditions those obligations are triggered due to the lack of clarity in termination terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17.3 EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Upon expiration or termination for any reason of this Agreement, the Franchisee's right to use the Burger King Marks and the Burger King System shall terminate. The Franchisee shall not thereafter identify itself as a Burger King franchisee or former Burger King franchisee or use, any of BKC's trade secrets, operating procedures, promotional materials, Burger King Marks or any marks confusingly similar. The Franchisee will immediately return to BKC the MOD Manual loaned to the Franchisee including any translations thereof, together with all other materials containing trade secrets, restaurant operating instructions or business practices of BKC. Where applicable, BKC shall be entitled to take all steps necessary for the cancellation of the entries of the Franchisee with the Registrar of Trademarks, or its equivalent authority, as a registered user without opposition or hindrance of the Franchisee. The Franchisee will, at the request and cost of BKC, cooperate in any such steps.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that termination is 'automatic without any further notice or opportunity to cure'. The changed text removes this phrase, implying that BKC might need to provide notice or an opportunity to cure. This directly contradicts the original intent of an immediate termination and introduces ambiguity regarding the termination process, especially considering section 17.1.1. This ambiguity then affects the enforcement of section 17.3, which details the effects of termination (e.g., ceasing to use Burger King Marks). If the termination is no longer automatically triggered by an Event of Default, it's unclear when the obligations in 17.3 are triggered, creating a conflict.
